title: Healthcheck always shows "starting" for quadlet service
body:
### Issue Description

I'm trying to setup some containers using quadlet and podman but the healthchecks are stuck at "starting" and never execute. It is basically the same thing that was discussed here https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/19381, but I definitely have systemd on my system. I am doing this on a NixOS machine.
```json
               "Health": {
                    "Status": "starting",
                    "FailingStreak": 0,
                    "Log": null
               },
```
It does work when I manually run `podman healthcheck run id` and it works if I run the same container just from my user with `podman run`. So it definitely is an issue with systemd.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Setup a NixOS
2. Create a Quadlet service for podman that includes a HealthCmd
3. Watch the status of that container never change


### Describe the results you received

The container never reaches a `healthy` state.

### Describe the results you expected

A container with a healthy state.

### podman info output

comments.body: Hi @Keyruu, can you share an example of such a Quadlet file?

Hi @ygalblum, of course!
```
[root@hati:~]# cat /etc/containers/systemd/immich-redis.container
[Container]
ContainerName=immich-redis
HealthCmd=redis-cli ping || exit 1
Image=redis:6.2-alpine@sha256:c5a607fb6e1bb15d32bbcf14db22787d19e428d59e31a5da67511b49bb0f1ccc
Network=immich.network
NetworkAlias=redis


[Service]
Environment=PATH=/run/wrappers/bin
Restart=always
TimeoutStartSec=900
User=root


[Unit]
Description=Podman container immich-redis
```
Thanks, I see that it refers to a Quadlet network: `immich.network` anything specific in this file or just an empty `[Network]` section?

Pretty standard I think:
```
[root@hati:~]# cat /etc/containers/systemd/immich.network
[Network]
Driver=bridge


[Service]
ExecStop=/nix/store/szxc93qlzmy33si8nvwnl33qn4ypf8qd-podman-5.3.1/bin/podman network rm systemd-immich


[Unit]
Description=Podman network immich
```

I just tried it on a Fedora 40 machine running podman 5.3.1 as well and from the looks of it, it seems to be working. I did have to be more explicit in the image:
```
Image=docker.io/library/redis:6.2-alpine@sha256:c5a607fb6e1bb15d32bbcf14db22787d19e428d59e31a5da67511b49bb0f1ccc
```
But, from then on:
```bash
$ sudo podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                                                                            COMMAND       CREATED        STATUS                  PORTS       NAMES
a696f4a34de0  docker.io/library/redis@sha256:c5a607fb6e1bb15d32bbcf14db22787d19e428d59e31a5da67511b49bb0f1ccc  redis-server  3 minutes ago  Up 3 minutes (healthy)  6379/tcp    immich-redis
```
```bash
$ systemctl status immich-redis.service
● immich-redis.service - Podman container immich-redis
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/containers/systemd/immich-redis.container; generated)
    Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/system/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
     Active: active (running) since Fri 2025-01-17 09:47:21 EST; 5min ago
   Main PID: 224960 (conmon)
      Tasks: 11 (limit: 4534)
     Memory: 3.7M (peak: 19.6M)
        CPU: 754ms
     CGroup: /system.slice/immich-redis.service
             ├─libpod-payload-a696f4a34de0cd8da5277a3a745eb60e5973bb0c52711b936b74cf22aee05ecd
             │ └─224962 "redis-server *:6379"
             └─runtime
               ├─224943 /usr/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns --config /run/containers/networks/aardvark-dns -p 53 run
               └─224960 /usr/bin/conmon --api-version 1 -c a696f4a34de0cd8da5277a3a745eb60e5973bb0c52711b936b74cf22aee05ecd -u a696f4a34de0cd8da5277a3a745eb60e5973bb0c52711b936b74cf22aee05ec>

Jan 17 09:47:21 lima-default immich-redis[224960]: 1:C 17 Jan 2025 14:47:21.281 # oO0OoO0OoO0Oo Redis is starting oO0OoO0OoO0Oo
```

```
sudo podman inspect immich-redis | jq .[0].State.Health
{
  "Status": "healthy",
  "FailingStreak": 0,
  "Log": [
    {
      "Start": "2025-01-17T09:49:10.656848096-05:00",
      "End": "2025-01-17T09:49:10.717579487-05:00",
      "ExitCode": 0,
      "Output": "PONG\n"
    },
    {
      "Start": "2025-01-17T09:49:13.45639401-05:00",
      "End": "2025-01-17T09:49:13.514529433-05:00",
      "ExitCode": 0,
      "Output": "PONG\n"
    }
  ]
}
```
Am I missing something?
I mean, I'm on NixOS but I don't know if that changes anything with the podman setup.

This is the whole `podman inspect`:

The issue here is that `Environment=PATH=/run/wrappers/bin` overwrites the default `$PATH` which contains `${pkgs.systemd}/bin`. This causes error when podman tries to run health command with `systemd-run`.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/0f04ba87bb7b2bdb20fe67705db6e428fd3dcdac/libpod/healthcheck_linux.go#L56

This env is generated by [quadlet-nix](https://github.com/SEIAROTg/quadlet-nix/) so I'm going to fix it there.

For podman, maybe this failure should be more obvious than containers being stuck in "starting"?

> The issue here is that `Environment=PATH=/run/wrappers/bin` overwrites the default `$PATH` which contains `${pkgs.systemd}/bin`. This causes error when podman tries to run health command with `systemd-run`.
> 
> [podman/libpod/healthcheck_linux.go](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/0f04ba87bb7b2bdb20fe67705db6e428fd3dcdac/libpod/healthcheck_linux.go#L56)
> 
> Line 56 in [0f04ba8](/containers/podman/commit/0f04ba87bb7b2bdb20fe67705db6e428fd3dcdac)
>  systemdRun := exec.Command("systemd-run", cmd...) 
> 
> This env is generated by [quadlet-nix](https://github.com/SEIAROTg/quadlet-nix/) so I'm going to fix it there.
> 
> For podman, maybe this failure should be more obvious than containers being stuck in "starting"?

Podman executes systemd-run and the CombinedOutput() will return an error if the binary does not exists or fails with exit code > 0 as such an error should be reported when that command fails.

Does nix overwrite systemd-run to not report errors? I don't see why podman would not report the error if we cannot run that command?

If the timer and service are created then we depend on systemd to execute them and to debug you would need to manually inspect the unit statel.

A simple reproduction would be:
```console
$ PATH='' /run/current-system/sw/bin/podman run -it --rm --health-cmd='curl localhost' nginx`
ERRO[0000]                                              
ERRO[0000] Unit 5bf3a4d5bfc00099ddda13bec8e8e89e8af6178aabdab5eb7366df747d53a8f5.service not found.

10-listen-on-ipv6-by-default.sh: info: Enabled listen on IPv6 in /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
```

So it does report some error, but:
- The message isn't really useful.
- The empty / unclear message can be easily overlooked when buried in journald.
- It doesn't change the state of health checking to error / unhealthy.

Think the empty line comes from here which should probably include `err` in the message.
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/0f04ba87bb7b2bdb20fe67705db6e428fd3dcdac/libpod/healthcheck_linux.go#L57-L59

Ah yes the error reporting is certainly broken there and needs to be fixed.
I would argue if we cannot start the timer it should return a hard error and not just log and move on because as you said that is easy to overlook.
